Home Economy Green Economy

Uzbekistan presents green energy experience in Tajikistan

Green Economy Materials 7 July 2026 15:56 (UTC +04:00)
  • Share on Facebook
  • Share on X
Niljan Bakhshaliyeva Read more

BAKU, Azerbaijan, July 7. Uzbekistan presented its experience in renewable energy development and green transition at the IV International Scientific and Practical Conference on the Current State and Prospects for the Development of Renewable Energy, held in Dushanbe, Tajikistan.

This was reflected in a statement by the Ministry of Energy of Uzbekistan.

The conference brought together scientists, industry experts, government officials, researchers, university representatives, and international organizations to discuss the latest developments in clean energy technologies.

The country's participation was organized under the regional Green Skills for a Green Economy in Central Asia project, implemented by the German Agency for International Cooperation.

Participants discussed global trends in renewable energy development, deployment of innovative technologies, digitalization of power systems, use of artificial intelligence in electricity grid management, and the future of solar and wind energy.

Tags: Central Asia green economy GIZ renewable energy Digitalization
